Released: 19 June 2026

This release of EDB Postgres® AI for CloudNativePG™ Cluster includes the following:

## Highlights

The `cluster` reference is now immutable on the `Database`, `Pooler`,
`Publication`, `Subscription`, and `ScheduledBackup` resources. Pointing one
of these objects at a different cluster has no well-defined semantics and
previously left the controllers in an inconsistent state; the update is now
rejected at the API server via a CEL validation rule.
([#10743](https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10743))

## Features

<table class="table w-100"><thead><tr><th>Description</th><th width="10%">Addresses</th></tr></thead><tbody>
<tr><td><details><summary>Primary <code>Lease</code> for safe primary election
</summary><hr/><p>introduced a Kubernetes <code>Lease</code> object (named after the cluster) that acts as
a mutex serializing primary promotion: the instance manager must hold the
lease before acting as primary and releases it on clean shutdown so replicas
can promote without waiting for the full TTL. Timings are configurable via the
new <code>.spec.primaryLease</code> stanza. The lease is a promotion gate, not a fence —
primary isolation remains responsible for fencing.</p>
</details></td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10627">#10627</a></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary><code>DatabaseRole</code> CRD for declarative role management
</summary><hr/><p>introduced a <code>DatabaseRole</code> custom resource that manages a PostgreSQL role as
a standalone Kubernetes object, instead of declaring it inline in the
<code>Cluster</code>'s <code>.spec.managed.roles</code> stanza. Each role gets its own lifecycle,
status, and RBAC, which suits GitOps workflows and lets role definitions live
next to the applications that own them. The spec reuses the same
<code>RoleConfiguration</code> structure as the inline method, so migrating a role is a
matter of moving the stanza into its own manifest. A
<code>databaseRoleReclaimPolicy</code> field (<code>retain</code>, the default, or <code>delete</code>)
controls what happens to the role when the resource is deleted, mirroring
persistent volumes.</p>
</details></td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/6155">#6155</a></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>TLS client certificates for declarative roles
</summary><hr/><p>a <code>DatabaseRole</code> can now include a <code>clientCertificate</code> block to have the
operator automatically generate and renew a TLS client certificate, signed by
the cluster's client CA and stored in a <code>&lt;databaserole-name&gt;-client-cert</code>
Secret. This enables password-free PostgreSQL <code>cert</code> authentication; the
Secret is cleaned up when the feature is disabled or the <code>DatabaseRole</code> is
deleted.</p>
</details></td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10896">#10896</a></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>PgBouncer image management via image catalogs
</summary><hr/><p>the <code>Pooler</code> resource can now reference an entry in an <code>ImageCatalog</code> or
<code>ClusterImageCatalog</code> through the new <code>spec.pgbouncer.imageCatalogRef</code> field,
centralizing PgBouncer image management. When a catalog entry is updated, all
referencing <code>Poolers</code> are automatically reconciled and roll out the new image
without any change to their spec. The resolved image is reported in
<code>status.image</code>, and a new <code>status.phase</code> (<code>active</code>, <code>paused</code>, <code>inactive</code>, or
<code>failed</code>) — also surfaced as a <code>Phase</code> column in <code>kubectl get pooler</code> —
summarizes the lifecycle.</p>
</details></td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10568">#10568</a></td></tr>
</tbody></table>


## Enhancements

<table class="table w-100"><thead><tr><th>Description</th><th width="10%">Addresses</th></tr></thead><tbody>
<tr><td><details><summary>Enabled <code>pg_upgrade</code> in-place major upgrades to PostgreSQL 19 or later for
clusters that use Image Volume extensions, building on the extension-path
support added to <code>pg_upgrade</code> in PostgreSQL 19.
</summary><hr/><p>During the upgrade <code>Job</code>, the source- and target-version extension images are
mounted side by side, so the old server keeps its libraries and a failed
upgrade reverts cleanly.</p>
</details></td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10366">#10366</a></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Added TLS support for the <code>Pooler</code> metrics endpoint via
<code>.spec.monitoring.tls.enabled</code>.
</summary><hr/><p>When enabled, the metrics server is served over HTTPS, reusing the certificate
and key from <code>.spec.pgbouncer.clientTLSSecret</code> and reloading it on every
handshake to support rotation without a restart; the generated <code>PodMonitor</code>
scrapes over <code>https</code> accordingly.</p>
</details></td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10466">#10466</a></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Added a label selector to the <code>Cluster</code> scale subresource (<code>status.selector</code>),
making a <code>Cluster</code> a valid <code>targetRef</code> for the Vertical Pod Autoscaler (VPA)
and Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA), which can now map a <code>Cluster</code> to its
instance pods.
</summary><hr/><p>Contributed by @sebv004.</p>
</details></td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/8996">#8996</a></td></tr>
<tr><td>The operator now emits a `Warning` `PrimaryStatusCheckFailed` event on the
`Cluster` when the primary pod is `Ready` from the kubelet perspective but the
operator's `/pg/status` check fails and failover is deferred, giving users
visibility into the deferral via `kubectl describe cluster`.
</td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10509">#10509</a></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Added the <code>ENABLE_WEBHOOK_NAMESPACE_SUFFIX</code> flag, which suffixes the
operator's webhook configuration names with <code>-&lt;OPERATOR_NAMESPACE&gt;</code> so that
multiple operator instances can coexist on the same cluster.
</summary><hr/><p>The operator only looks up these configurations; users must create and
maintain them. Contributed by @maxlengdell.</p>
</details></td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10420">#10420</a></td></tr>
<tr><td>The operator now reloads a CNPG-i plugin automatically when its pods are
rolled: it watches the `EndpointSlices` backing plugin `Services` and
re-enqueues every cluster using the plugin once the new pods become `Ready`,
so an upgraded plugin is picked up without waiting for the next resync.
</td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10836">#10836</a></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Instance serial numbers are now assigned by reusing the lowest free slot among
existing instance names, instead of always incrementing a global counter.
</summary><hr/><p>Pod and PVC names stay stable across instance recreation (for example, an
instance recreated after a node drain comes back with the same name), and
serials freed by deleted instances are reclaimed. A new <code>Initialized</code> cluster
condition reports whether the cluster has completed its first bootstrap, and
<code>status.latestGeneratedNode</code> is deprecated: it is no longer written, but is
preserved on the CRD for backward compatibility.</p>
</details></td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10548">#10548</a></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Defaulting and validation now run during reconciliation as a fallback when
admission webhooks are unavailable, or configured to ignore failures, so the
operator no longer reconciles invalid or incomplete specs.
</summary><hr/><p>Missing defaults are applied directly, and validation failures are surfaced in
the resource status instead of failing silently later.</p>
</details></td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10874">#10874</a></td></tr>
</tbody></table>


## Security Fixes

<table class="table w-100"><thead><tr><th>Description</th><th width="10%">Addresses</th></tr></thead><tbody>
<tr><td><details><summary><code>search_path</code> pinning on operator-issued connections
</summary><hr/><p>a database owner could plant overloaded built-in operators in the <code>public</code>
schema and alter the <code>search_path</code> so that operator introspection probes,
running as the cluster superuser, resolved those overloads before <code>pg_catalog</code>
— a <code>CWE-426</code> privilege-escalation chain (same class as <code>CVE-2018-1058</code>) that
could lead to in-pod RCE via <code>COPY ... FROM PROGRAM</code>. The operator now pins
<code>search_path = pg_catalog, public, pg_temp</code> on every pooled connection so it
ships in the startup message and takes precedence over tenant-controlled
defaults.</p>
</details></td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10774">#10774</a></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Authenticated operator-to-instance-manager calls
</summary><hr/><p>the instance manager's remote webserver previously accepted unauthenticated
requests, so any process on the pod network could trigger backups, instance
manager upgrades, or WAL archival. The operator now generates an in-memory
ECDSA P-256 client certificate at startup and reconciles its SHA-256
fingerprint into the cluster status; the instance manager rejects requests to
sensitive endpoints that do not present a matching certificate.</p>
</details></td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10579">#10579</a></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Operator-side SCRAM-SHA-256 password encoding
</summary><hr/><p>the operator now SCRAM-SHA-256 encodes cleartext role passwords before issuing
<code>CREATE</code>/<code>ALTER ROLE ... PASSWORD</code>, so the literal PostgreSQL parses (and that
extensions such as <code>pg_stat_statements</code> or <code>pgaudit</code> may capture) is the SCRAM
verifier rather than the cleartext secret. Pre-hashed (MD5 or SCRAM) values
are forwarded unchanged, and the per-Secret annotation
<code>k8s.enterprisedb.io/passwordPassthrough: &quot;enabled&quot;</code> opts out.</p>
</details></td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10724">#10724</a></td></tr>
</tbody></table>


## Changes

<table class="table w-100"><thead><tr><th>Description</th><th width="10%">Addresses</th></tr></thead><tbody>
<tr><td>Added support for Kubernetes 1.36.
</td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10900">#10900</a></td></tr>
<tr><td>Updated the default PostgreSQL version to 18.4.
</td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10719">#10719</a></td></tr>
<tr><td>Updated the Kubernetes versions used to test the operator on public cloud
providers.
</td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10720">#10720</a>, <a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10563">#10563</a></td></tr>
</tbody></table>


## Bug Fixes

<table class="table w-100"><thead><tr><th>Description</th><th width="10%">Addresses</th></tr></thead><tbody>
<tr><td>Fixed declarative `Database`, `Publication`, and `Subscription` objects
reporting a stale primary-side status forever after their cluster was demoted
to a replica; the controller now re-checks the replica condition and watches
the `Cluster` so a demotion is detected promptly.
</td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10871">#10871</a></td></tr>
<tr><td>Fixed non-sequential pod names (for example `-1`, `-3`) caused by the instance
serial counter being advanced before the corresponding `Job` and PVCs were
created; the bump is now persisted only after those resources exist.
</td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10491">#10491</a></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed deletion of a <code>Database</code>, <code>Publication</code>, or <code>Subscription</code> getting stuck
in <code>Terminating</code> on a replica cluster, where the replica gate ran before the
finalizer reconciler and the finalizer was never released.
</summary><hr/><p>On a replica the PostgreSQL object is left to the primary cluster.</p>
</details></td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10853">#10853</a></td></tr>
<tr><td>Fixed a conflicting duplicate `Database` or `Subscription` with a `delete`
reclaim policy dropping the PostgreSQL object owned by the surviving CR; the
drop is now gated on a recorded reconciliation.
</td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10870">#10870</a></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed the <code>postgres</code> superuser being left locked out after superuser access
was disabled and then re-enabled, because the cached secret version was not
invalidated and the password was never re-applied.
</summary><hr/><p>Diagnosed by @mhartmann-jaconi.</p>
</details></td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10834">#10834</a></td></tr>
<tr><td>Fixed backups getting stuck in the `started` phase when the instance manager
running them was restarted (for example by the in-place upgrade following an
operator upgrade) before the backup reached `running`; the reconciliation is
now rescheduled so the lost session is detected.
</td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10859">#10859</a></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed <code>exec</code>/<code>attach</code> streaming to negotiate WebSocket with a SPDY fallback,
restoring compatibility both with Kubernetes versions that have removed SPDY
and with platforms such as OpenShift that reject WebSocket exec upgrades.
</summary><hr/><p>Contributed by @bartscheers.</p>
</details></td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10876">#10876</a>, <a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10933">#10933</a></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed resource leaks when concurrent <code>Backup</code> objects raced: backups now run
in strict creation-time order, so an already-executing backup is never
preempted by a newer one and its replication slot and PostgreSQL session are
no longer orphaned on the primary.
</summary><hr/><p>Contributed by @GabriFedi97.</p>
</details></td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10747">#10747</a></td></tr>
<tr><td>Fixed role reconciliation clearing the password on a PostgreSQL role when the
referenced Secret could not be fetched; the role is now left untouched until
the Secret becomes available, and per-action errors are aggregated for better
visibility.
</td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10053">#10053</a></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed a bootstrap failure where a metrics-exporter setup error (commonly a
duplicate-key race with the controller) rolled back <code>streaming_replica</code>
creation and wedged replica joins.
</summary><hr/><p>The metrics-exporter step now runs in a separate transaction. Contributed by
@BlaiseAntony.</p>
</details></td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10749">#10749</a></td></tr>
<tr><td>Fixed a `ScheduledBackup` controller loop that occurred when a `Backup` was
created but its status patch never landed; the controller now adopts an
existing `Backup` for the next iteration instead of looping on
`AlreadyExists`.
</td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10612">#10612</a></td></tr>
<tr><td>Fixed a nil-pointer panic when reconciling a `Pooler` whose `Cluster` has been
deleted.
</td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10667">#10667</a></td></tr>
<tr><td>Fixed bootstrap log handling so that all named log pipes (`postgres`,
`postgres.csv`, and `postgres.json`) get consumers during
`WithActiveInstance`, preventing regular files from being created in place of
the named pipes.
</td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10043">#10043</a></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed generation of invalid IPv6 URLs by wrapping the address in square
brackets.
</summary><hr/><p>Contributed by @Infinoid.</p>
</details></td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10682">#10682</a></td></tr>
<tr><td>Fixed an external cluster plugin still being treated as active when its
configuration set `enabled: false`.
</td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10932">#10932</a></td></tr>
<tr><td><details><summary>Fixed a race during bootstrap recovery from an object store where the restore
job could read a stale <code>Cluster</code> (primary not yet recorded and timeline still
unset) and have its <code>.history</code> files rejected by the split-brain guard.
</summary><hr/><p>When this happened, recovery stopped at the base backup's timeline and
silently dropped transactions committed on later timelines. History files are
now allowed while the cluster timeline is unset. Contributed by @dennispidun.</p>
</details></td><td><a href="https://github.com/cloudnative-pg/cloudnative-pg/pull/10818">#10818</a></td></tr>
</tbody></table>
